zh.Passe-d'Anor, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais,: 法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德帕-達亞諾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Wouter the Second Van Chatillan於1146年出生在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德帕-達亞諾時，他的父親Hendrik Van Chatillan25歲，母親Elizabeth Van Crepy25歲。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.Passe-d'Anor, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais,: 法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德帕-達亞諾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Wouter the Second Van Chatillan于1146年出生在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德帕-達亞諾时，他的父亲Hendrik Van Chatillan25岁，母亲Elizabeth Van Crepy25岁。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Wouter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

nameMeanings.name: the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Dorothea  , also used independently.

nameMeanings.name: Second
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Sicard , reflecting the characteristic Canadian and American French rounding of the -ard ending. Compare Secor and Sicord .
History: The Secords are descendants of Ambroise Sicard, a Huguenot refugee from France (see   Sicard  ).

variants.name: Sicard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adrien, Albertine, Aude, Benoit, Camille, Elzear, Francois, Jacques, Jean-Paul, Marcel, Monique, Numa.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Sighard, composed of the elements sig ‘victory’ and hard ‘hard, strong’. Compare Seacord , Secor , Secord , Secore , Secoy , and Sicord .

History: Jean Sicard from Mornac-sur-Seudre in Charente-Maritime, France, married Catherine Lauzon in Montreal, QC, in 1681. Jean-Baptiste Sicard, sieur de Carufel (see Carufel ), from Castres in Tarn, France, married Geneviève Raté in Saint-Pierre, Île d'Orléans, QC, in 1694. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its altered forms Sicord and Seacord) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. The Huguenot ancestor was Ambroise Sicard from France, who arrived with his wife and children in NY in the 1680s via England. His descendants (also) bear the surnames Seacord, Secor, Secord, and Secoy.

en.lifeSummary: When Wouter the Second Van Chatillan was born in 1146, in Passe-d'Anor, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, his father, Hendrik Van Chatillan, was 25 and his mother, Elizabeth Van Crepy, was 25. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Wouter the Second Van Chatillan est né en 1146, à Passe-d'Anor, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, son père, Hendrik Van Chatillan, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Van Crepy, avait 25 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Wouter the Second Van Chatillan 1146 in Passe-d'Anor, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hendrik Van Chatillan 25 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Van Crepy 25. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Wouter the Second Van Chatillan è nato il nel 1146 nel Passe-d'Anor, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, il suo padre, Hendrik Van Chatillan, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Van Crepy, aveva 25 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Wouter the Second Van Chatillanが1146年にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードプアセ-ド'アノーで生まれた時，父Hendrik Van Chatillanは25歳，母Elizabeth Van Crepyは25歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Wouter the Second Van Chatillan(이)가 1146년 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 파세-드'아너에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hendrik Van Chatillan(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Van Crepy(은)는 25세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Wouter the Second Van Chatillan nasceu em 1146, em Passe-d'Anor, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, seu pai, Hendrik Van Chatillan, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Van Crepy, tinha 25 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Wouter the Second Van Chatillan родился в 1146 г. в местности пассе-д'анор, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция, его отцу по имени Hendrik Van Chatillan было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Van Crepy, 25. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Wouter the Second Van Chatillan nació en 1146, en Passe-d'Anor, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, su papá, Hendrik Van Chatillan, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Van Crepy, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
